mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Improvements in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during liquidity crunches or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over 1-year rolling windows in recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals collapsing construction demand and reduced household wealth effects, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered months or the housing signal shows no predictive power after controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, supporting higher corporate revenues and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when wage growth outpaces productivity, or forward returns are negative in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and increases discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or inflation spikes fail to predict negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal fails during stagflation periods or forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters with positive GDP growth.
